content_blocks.text:
## 2. Regulatory Framework and Scope

### 2.1 Statutory and Regulatory Basis

This Policy is established by {firm_name} (hereinafter, "the Company" or "the Reporting Entity" or "the RE") in compliance with the following statutory and regulatory framework governing its operations as a Virtual Digital Asset Service Provider ("VDA SP") and Reporting Entity in India:

**Primary Statute:**
- Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002 ("PMLA"), including Section 2(1)(sa)(vi) [designated business or profession], Section 2(1)(wa) [reporting entity], Section 3 [offence of money-laundering], Chapter IV [obligations of banking companies, financial institutions and intermediaries], Section 12 [reporting entity to maintain records], Section 12A [access to information], Section 12(2) [confidentiality of information furnished], and Section 13 [powers of Director to impose fine].

**Subordinate Rules:**
- Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005 ("PML Rules" or "PMLR"), including Rule 2(1)(ba) [Designated Director], Rule 2(1)(cba) [Group], Rule 2(1)(cf) [Non-Profit Organisation], Rule 2(1)(db) [Politically Exposed Person], Rule 2(1)(f) [Principal Officer], Rule 3 [transactions to be reported], Rule 3A [policies to be formulated on a Group basis], Rule 4 [records to be maintained], Rule 5 [maintenance and preservation of records], Rule 7 [furnishing of information], Rule 7(1) [intimation of Designated Director and Principal Officer details], Rule 7(2) [furnishing of information by Principal Officer], Rule 7(3) [internal mechanism], Rule 8(2) [furnishing of suspicious transaction information], Rule 8(6) [confidentiality], Rule 9 [client due diligence], Rule 9(3) [beneficial owner verification], Rule 9(9A) [DARPAN Portal registration for Non-Profit Organisations], Rule 9(12) [ongoing due diligence], Rule 9(13) [risk assessment], Rule 9(14) [policies and procedures], and Rule 10(3) [suspicious transaction reporting upon termination of relationship].

**Designating Notifications:**
- Ministry of Finance Notification S.O. 1072(E) dated March 7, 2023, designating activities relating to virtual digital assets under Section 2(1)(sa)(vi) of PMLA as a designated business or profession.
- Ministry of Finance Notification dated July 4, 2023, reaffirming FIU-IND registration as a prerequisite for compliance by VDA SPs under PMLA.
- Ministry of Finance Notification S.O. 4877(E) dated November 9, 2023, notifying FIU-IND as the regulator for VDA SPs.

**Operational Guidance (Primary):**
- FIU-IND AML & CFT Guidelines for Reporting Entities Providing Services Related to Virtual Digital Assets dated January 8, 2026 (hereinafter, "2026 FIU-IND Guidelines" or "the Guidelines"). The 2026 Guidelines supersede the AML & CFT Guidelines dated March 10, 2023 and consolidate the Circular for Registration of VDA SPs dated September 15, 2025 and the Guidance for Principal Officer (Minimum Requirements and Qualifications) dated February 25, 2025.

**Adjacent Tax Framework Applicable to VDA SPs:**
- Section 115BBH, Income Tax Act, 1961 (taxation of income from transfer of virtual digital assets at thirty percent plus applicable cess and surcharge).
- Section 194S, Income Tax Act, 1961 (one percent TDS on transfer of virtual digital assets, with thresholds of ₹10,000 in general and ₹50,000 for specified persons).
- Section 509, Income Tax Act, 1961 (effective April 1, 2026 — reporting obligations for crypto-asset transactions, with applicable penalties for failure to file).
- Forms 26Q, 26QE, and 26QF (TDS challan-cum-statement forms for VDA transactions).
- Schedule VDA in Income Tax Return forms.

**Cybersecurity Framework Applicable to VDA SPs:**
- Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) Directions dated April 28, 2022, issued under Section 70B(6) of the Information Technology Act, 2000.

**Sanctions and Counter-Terrorism Framework:**
- United Nations Security Council Resolutions (UNSCRs) relating to terrorism, terrorist financing, and proliferation of weapons of mass destruction and its financing.
- Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967 (UAPA) and designations of terrorists and terrorist organisations thereunder.
- Weapons of Mass Destruction and their Delivery Systems (Prohibition of Unlawful Activities) Act, 2005 (WMDA).

**Forward-Looking Obligations:**
- OECD Crypto Asset Reporting Framework (CARF), adopted by India, with implementation reporting expected by April 2027 (subject to publication of Indian implementing rules).

**International Standards Referenced:**
- Financial Action Task Force (FATF) Recommendations, particularly Recommendation 1 (risk-based approach), Recommendation 10 (customer due diligence), Recommendation 11 (record-keeping), Recommendation 16 (wire transfers / the Travel Rule), and Recommendation 20 (reporting of suspicious transactions).

### 2.2 Scope and Applicability

This Policy applies to:

(a) The Company in its entirety, including all branches, subsidiaries, affiliates, and operational units, on a Group basis where applicable in accordance with Rule 3A of the PML Rules and Paragraph 3.4.3(a) of the 2026 Guidelines;

(b) All directors, officers, employees (permanent as well as contractual), contractors, consultants, agents, and representatives of the Company, irrespective of seniority or function, in accordance with Section 12(2) and Section 12A(3) of the PMLA read with Rule 8(6) of the PML Rules;

(c) All products, services, and business lines offered by the Company, including but not limited to: {applicable_services};

(d) All customers of the Company, whether individual, legal person (other than natural person), or non-profit organisation, and irrespective of customer geography;

(e) All counterparties with whom the Company transacts, including other VDA SPs (whether registered with FIU-IND or otherwise), financial institutions, and any other counterparty types.

### 2.3 Reporting Entity Status and Activity-Based Obligations

The Company is a Reporting Entity under Section 2(1)(wa) of PMLA, by virtue of carrying on designated business or profession as notified by the Ministry of Finance vide Notification S.O. 1072(E) dated March 7, 2023. The Company is registered with FIU-IND as a Reporting Entity. The Company's FIU-IND Reporting Entity Identification Number is: [INSERT RE-ID].

All obligations specified in this Policy are mandatory consequences of the Company's status as a Reporting Entity. In accordance with the activity-based nature of these obligations as confirmed in Paragraph 7.3 of the 2026 Guidelines, the obligations apply irrespective of the Company's physical presence or absence in any specific jurisdiction within India, where the Company engages in notified VDA activities relating to Indian users.

### 2.4 Cross-Regulatory Context

The Company recognises that its operations are subject to oversight by multiple regulators and authorities in addition to FIU-IND, including:

- Reserve Bank of India (RBI), particularly with respect to banking relationships and payment system integrations.
- Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI), with respect to any VDAs that may qualify as securities under applicable jurisdiction.
- Income Tax Department, with respect to TDS obligations under Section 194S, reporting obligations under Section 509, and related compliance.
- Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), with respect to information technology and platform compliance, including URL/app-level enforcement under PMLA referrals.
- Computer Emergency Response Team – India (CERT-In), with respect to cybersecurity audit certification and incident reporting obligations.
- Enforcement Directorate (ED), with respect to PMLA-related investigations and asset attachment proceedings.

The Company maintains a coordinated compliance posture across these regulators, with FIU-IND obligations under PMLA as the foundation of its financial crime compliance framework.
